summary: The child has severe beta-thalassemia indicated by very elevated HbF; thus, a typical finding on their blood film includes microcytic, hypochromic red blood cells indicative of chronic hemolysis and ineffective hematopoiesis. This contrasts sharply with conditions such as sickle cell disease which do not present similarly distinctive morphological abnormalities under these specific circumstances. ```

This contrasts sharply with conditions such as sickle cell disease which do not present similarly distinctive morphological abnormalities under these specific circumstances. ```. Answer choice: B
